purpose: Compact visual-support map for Batch 1 service pages. Use this to plan one strong visual moment per service page without redesigning the service system.

globalRules.visualStrategy: Cloudinary-first for real proof. Pexels/stock only for light supporting atmosphere where no real proof image exists.
antiPatterns: generic stock hero imagery replacing real proof
multiple competing galleries on one service page
using logos as the main proof image if a real screen or deliverable exists
pixelated raster logos as hero media
autoplay carousels or motion-heavy proof sections

recommendedAssets: dashboard or system screenshot from Graston engagement
high-resolution product or site screen only if it clearly represents strategic oversight
fractional-cmo.captionDirection: Strategic leadership is visible in how the whole system is structured, not just in one campaign output.

recommendedAssets: homepage or service-page screen from Pike Medical
device-framed site screen with clear UI hierarchy
before/after crop only if both sides are high quality and aligned
website-strategy.captionDirection: The issue is not only how the site looks, but how clearly it earns trust and moves the right visitor forward.

recommendedAssets: clean UI screen that shows operational structure rather than marketing fluff
crm-architecture.captionDirection: A CRM should be a system you can run from, not just software you happen to have.

recommendedAssets: service page or local landing screen
map/GBP-supporting screen only if visually clean
trust-signal or review-led screen if it represents real local proof
local-seo.captionDirection: Local visibility only matters when it leads to trust and action, not just rankings.

recommendedAssets: high-clarity UI state showing action path
mobile-first crop if conversion behavior is strongest there
conversion-optimization.captionDirection: The goal is not more activity on the page — it is a clearer path to action.
